goal: Replicate the rk3576-home-assistant-voice Home Assistant local voice stack for AX650 board-side Docker according to plan.md.

requirement_alignment.goal: Create an AX650 implementation that preserves the Home Assistant integration experience from rk3576-home-assistant-voice: Wyoming STT on 10300, Wyoming TTS on 10200, openWakeWord on 10400, OpenAI-compatible LLM on 8001, and optional Home Assistant profile.
non_goals: Do not migrate RKNN or RKLLM artifacts from the RK3576 repository.
Do not commit large model files or .axmodel assets.
Do not deploy or run the stack on an AX650 board in the first iteration.
Do not publish Docker images or merge a PR automatically.
expected_behavior: ASR adapter accepts Wyoming audio events and forwards a temporary WAV to ax_asr_api's OpenAI-style transcription endpoint.
TTS adapter accepts Wyoming synthesis events and streams WAV audio returned by ax_tts_api as Wyoming audio events.
Compose exposes the same user-facing Home Assistant ports as the RK3576 stack.
README documents AX650 setup, Home Assistant integration, model placement, and validation commands.
constraints_and_risks: AX650 runtime services may require privileged Docker access and /dev bind mounts.
Exact ax_asr_api, ax_tts_api, and axllm package/runtime installation details may need later board validation.
Local validation uses adapter unit tests with fake HTTP services, not real AX650 inference.
acceptance_checks: Adapter unit tests cover Wyoming-to-HTTP and HTTP-to-Wyoming event conversion.
README describes service ports and Home Assistant setup.
requirement_alignment.validation_approach: Run python -m pytest -q and docker compose config locally; reserve AX650 board validation for a follow-up iteration.
requirement_alignment.first_iteration_scope: Implement the AX650 project skeleton, Docker Compose services, ASR/TTS Wyoming HTTP adapters, local tests, download placeholders, and documentation.
likely_follow_up_iterations: Validate and adjust Dockerfiles against real ax_asr_api, ax_tts_api, and axllm packaging on AX650.
Run board-side end-to-end checks with real models.
Add CI or image publishing if desired.

issues.title: feat[1]: scaffold AX650 Home Assistant voice stack
acceptance_checks: Project directories and compose services exist for asr, tts, llm, openwakeword, and optional Home Assistant.
ASR adapter converts Wyoming audio events into an OpenAI-style transcription HTTP request and returns Transcript.
TTS adapter converts Wyoming synthesis requests into OpenAI-style speech HTTP requests and streams returned WAV audio.
Unit tests verify ASR/TTS adapter event conversion using fake HTTP clients.
README documents AX650 setup, ports, model placement, and Home Assistant integration.

rounds.implementation_summary: Scaffolded the AX650 Docker Compose voice stack and implemented locally tested ASR/TTS Wyoming adapters that bridge to AXERA OpenAI-style HTTP APIs.

validation.evidence: Parsed services: asr, tts, openwakeword, llm, homeassistant profile

rounds.test_summary: Passed locally: pytest reported 6 passed; docker-compose config parsed all services. The Docker Compose plugin command docker compose config is unavailable in this environment.
rounds.commit: HEAD commit for iteration 1; final hash reported by git/PR
rounds.commit_message: feat(iter-1): scaffold AX650 Home Assistant voice stack (#1)

assumptions: The target PR base branch is main.
GitHub CLI authentication is available for issues and PR creation.
The first iteration should be reviewable as a single commit.
plan.md and AGENTS.md predate this build-project run and will not be staged unless explicitly needed.
